🚀 Push & Publish to GitHub Pages

From your local folder → live website in ~5 minutes

PHASE 1

Create a GitHub Repository

1
Go to GitHub and create a new repo
Open github.com/new
• Repository name: devops-interview-guide
• Visibility: Public (required for free GitHub Pages)
• Do NOT tick "Add a README" — your local files already have one
• Click Create repository
PHASE 2

Connect & Push from Your Machine

2
Open a terminal in the repo folder
In VS Code: open the folder, then press Ctrl + ` to open the terminal.
Or open PowerShell and navigate there:
cd C:\Users\PoornaGottimukkula\Documents\new-nob\devops-interview-guide
3
Check git is initialised (it should be)
git status # Should show: On branch main — with untracked/modified files
4
Add your GitHub repo as the remote
Replace your-username with your actual GitHub username:
git remote add origin https://github.com/your-username/devops-interview-guide.git # If remote already exists, update it instead: git remote set-url origin https://github.com/your-username/devops-interview-guide.git
5
Stage all files, commit, and push
git add . git commit -m "feat: add GitHub Pages site with Jekyll theme" git branch -M main git push -u origin main
💡
If prompted for credentials, enter your GitHub username and a Personal Access Token (not your password). Create one at github.com/settings/tokens → Classic token → tick repo scope.
PHASE 3

Enable GitHub Pages

6
Open repo Settings → Pages
In your browser go to:
https://github.com/your-username/devops-interview-guide/settings/pages
📸 Settings → Pages (left sidebar under "Code and automation")
7
Set Source to "GitHub Actions"
Under Build and deployment → Source:
Select GitHub Actions from the dropdown (not "Deploy from a branch")
Click Save
📸 Source dropdown → GitHub Actions → Save
PHASE 4

Update Your Site URL in _config.yml

8
Edit _config.yml with your real GitHub username
Open devops-interview-guide/_config.yml and change these two lines:
# Change these two lines: url: "https://your-username.github.io" baseurl: "/devops-interview-guide" # Also add (optional but useful): github_username: your-username
9
Commit and push the config change
git add _config.yml git commit -m "config: set GitHub Pages URL" git push
PHASE 5

Watch It Deploy & Go Live

10
Watch the Actions workflow run
Go to your repo → Actions tab
You'll see "Deploy GitHub Pages" workflow running (~60 seconds)
✅ Green tick = deploy succeeded
📸 Actions → Deploy GitHub Pages → green ✓
11
Open your live site 🎉
Your site is now live at:
https://your-username.github.io/devops-interview-guide
🔄
Every future git push to main automatically rebuilds and redeploys the site. No manual steps needed after this.
HELP

Common Issues

?
Actions workflow fails with "bundler error"
Run locally first to check Gemfile is valid:
# Install Ruby first if needed: https://rubyinstaller.org gem install bundler bundle install bundle exec jekyll build
?
Site loads but CSS is missing / links broken
Make sure baseurl in _config.yml exactly matches your repo name (case-sensitive).
# Repo name is "devops-interview-guide" → baseurl must be: baseurl: "/devops-interview-guide"
?
"Permission denied" on git push
You need a Personal Access Token. Create one at github.com/settings/tokens/new → Classic → tick repo → copy the token → use it as your password when pushing.

📋 Quick Reference — Commands to Run

cd C:\Users\PoornaGottimukkula\Documents\new-nob\devops-interview-guide
git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/devops-interview-guide.git
git add . && git commit -m "feat: add GitHub Pages site"
git branch -M main && git push -u origin main
GitHub → Settings → Pages → Source: GitHub Actions
Edit _config.yml → set url + baseurl → push again
Wait ~60s → visit https://your-username.github.io/devops-interview-guide
Made with IBM Bob
